_comment_top: Example dispatch config for opencode_dispatch.py. Copy to <project>/.colosseum/dispatch.json and edit. Schema described in colosseum/scripts/opencode_dispatch.py.
_comment_canonical_panel: CANONICAL 5-VOICE PANEL for adversarial spec review. Each voice is pinned to the strongest most-recent variant of its model family with max thinking enabled. The Claude voice runs in-harness via the colosseum-spec-adversary Agent subagent (NOT through this script) — dispatch claude-agent separately alongside opencode_dispatch.py. The four voices below are the OpenCode-dispatched roster. For Lean-specific tasks substitute leanstral for one of these voices (lmstudio/leanstral-2603).

voices.note: ChatGPT latest via OpenCode direct openai provider. Invoked at --variant max. Verify the model id against `opencode models openai` if the gpt-5.1-thinking string drifts.

Moonshot Kimi via Burnt gateway. Frontier tier; ReAct loop produces 6.8-15K-char per-slice reports. Reasoning toggle is gateway-controlled; if the gateway gains a kimi-thinking route, update this entry.
DeepSeek V4 Flash via local ds4 provider (DwarfStar4, http://127.0.0.1:8000). 400K context, reasoning on, q2-q4-imatrix quant. Invoked at --variant max.
Gemini 3 Pro (strongest current Gemini) via OpenCode direct google provider. Invoked at --variant max. Verify the model id against `opencode models google` if the gemini-3-pro string drifts.

_comment_claude_voice: The fifth voice in the canonical panel is Claude Opus 4.x (most recent: 4.7) dispatched in-harness as `claude-agent` via the colosseum-spec-adversary Agent subagent. That voice does NOT run through opencode_dispatch.py — the Agent tool invocation happens from the Claude Code session that owns the adversarial pass. See SKILL Step 4 Mode 2.
_comment_excluded_voices: Voices known to fail adversarial spec review: glm-4-7-flash (degenerate loops at 30B 'flash' tier); goedel-prover-v2-32b and other theorem-prover specialists (treat prompts as proof goals instead of attack targets — use at verify-pyramid layer instead). leanstral-2603 belongs in Lean-specific verification work, not general adversarial spec review — substitute only when the spec is a Lean theorem.
_comment_dispatch_invocation: opencode_dispatch.py passes --format default and --dangerously-skip-permissions to each `opencode run` call. To pin --variant max per voice, modify the `cmd` list in opencode_dispatch.py:dispatch_one to append the --variant flag, or set the variant in each voice's opencode.jsonc model definition.
_comment_slices: Slice plan. Each slice is one (voice, slice) call. headers[] are the markdown header ranges the agent should read from the target spec. attack_emphasis is the per-slice attack-category hint. Replace this example with your project's actual sections.

slices.name: scope
slices.label: System identity, non-goals, external contracts
slices.attack_emphasis: Identity overreach; non-goals that should be in scope; caller-contract clauses that admit clients the doc intends to exclude.

slices.name: behaviors-core
slices.label: Worked examples — happy path, boundary, edge, explicit failures
slices.attack_emphasis: Worked-example arithmetic; edge-case completeness; explicit-failure listing completeness; happy-path implicit assumptions.

slices.name: invariants
slices.label: Structural and behavioral invariants
slices.attack_emphasis: Pointwise evaluability of state invariants; temporal-tag correctness; coverage gaps; derived-flag soundness.

_comment_context_appendix: Optional shared-context block prepended to every call. Use for type signatures, invariant labels, or cross-section glossary entries that are load-bearing but not under attack. Mark clearly as read-only / not-to-be-attacked.
context_appendix:
=== CONTEXT APPENDIX (read-only — DO NOT ATTACK; these sections are attacked in their own invocations) ===

System under review: <your system name and one-line description>.
Intent doc: version <X.Y.Z>.

=== END CONTEXT APPENDIX ===
_comment_timeouts: per_call_timeout: seconds budget per (voice, slice) call. Local reasoning models on consumer hardware can take 10-25 min per slice. max_retries: per-call retry budget on truncated stubs or HTTP errors.
